title: Peach Cobbler
description: Warm spiced peaches topped with a soft, buttery biscuit crust. A Southern classic that's pure comfort in a dish.

ingredients.note: peeled, or canned peaches in juice drained

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a small 8x8 inch baking dish.
If using fresh peaches: steam peeled, sliced peaches for 8–10 minutes until very tender. If using canned: heat in saucepan until soft, about 5 minutes.
Mash softened peaches with a fork until pieces are small and soft. Stir in sugar, cinnamon, and lemon juice. Spread in the baking dish.
Whisk together flour, baking powder, sugar, salt, milk, and butter until a smooth batter forms.
Spoon batter in small dollops over the peaches. Do not spread — it will settle while baking.
Bake for 20 minutes until the biscuit topping is just set but still very soft and moist — not browned or crispy.
Remove from oven. Spoon 2 tbsp warm water or reserved peach juice over the top immediately to soak the biscuit.
Cool for 10 minutes. Spoon into serving dishes, mixing biscuit with peach filling. Serve with ice cream if desired.

notes: Canned peaches in juice are a reliable, consistent option when fresh peaches are out of season. Always remove peach skins — even after cooking, the skins can be tough.
